en.extract:
The banded cotinga (Cotinga maculata) is a species of bird in the family Cotingidae. It is endemic to southeastern Brazil.
Its natural habitat is tropical moist lowland forests. It is threatened by habitat loss as its population is estimated to number between 250 and 999 mature individuals.


Description
Birds are around 20 cm long, and males are a bright blue with a black spotted back. The throat and belly are bright purple with a blue band across the chest. Females are a dull brown with some white mottling. The primaries are modified to produce a slight whir during display.


Distribution
The majority of the species is concentrated in the state of Bahia, with occasional records from the nearby state of Minas Gerais. It most likely once occurred in Rio de Janeiro and Espírito Santo, but has since been extirpated.


Ecology and behavior
It inhabits the canopy of the lowland Atlantic forest, and has a diet of seeds, berries, especially from Byrsonima sericea and ficuses, fruit, caterpillars, and other insects. It shares its habitat with other endemic birds, such as the saffron toucanet and pin-tailed manakin. The banded cotinga is not migratory and its nest is a simple cup.


Conservation
The greatest threat to the banded cotinga's survival is deforestation. Continued habitat fragmentation has also complicated matters, sending populations into a sharp decline. Some protected areas, such as the RPPN Estação Veracel and Reserva Serra Bonita, are now thought to serve as bastions for this species.


References


External links
BirdLife Species Factsheet

The '''banded cotinga''' ('''''Cotinga maculata''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Cotingidae]]. It is [[endemic]] to southeastern [[Brazil]].

Its natural [[habitat]] is tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]] as its population is estimated to number between 250 and 999 mature individuals.<ref name="datazone">{{cite web |title=Banded Cotinga *Cotinga maculata* |url=https://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/banded-cotinga-cotinga-maculata/text |website=Birdlife Datazone |publisher=Birdlife |access-date=12 October 2020}}</ref>

==Description==
Birds are around 20 cm long, and males are a bright blue with a black spotted back. The throat and belly are bright purple with a blue band across the chest. Females are a dull brown with some white mottling.<ref name="datazone"/> The [[Primary feathers|primaries]] are modified to produce a slight whir during display.<ref name="abc"/>

==Distribution==
The majority of the species is concentrated in the state of [[Bahia]], with occasional records from the nearby state of [[Minas Gerais]]. It most likely once occurred in [[Rio de Janeiro (state)|Rio de Janeiro]] and [[Espírito Santo]], but has since been extirpated.

==Ecology and behavior==
It inhabits the canopy of the lowland [[Atlantic forest]], and has a diet of seeds, berries, especially from ''[[Byrsonima sericea]]'' and [[Ficus|ficuses]],<ref name="datazone"/> fruit, caterpillars, and other insects. It shares its habitat with other endemic birds, such as the [[saffron toucanet]] and [[pin-tailed manakin]].<ref name="abc">{{cite web |title=Banded Cotinga |url=https://abcbirds.org/bird/banded-cotinga/ |website=ABCBirds |publisher=American Bird Conservancy |access-date=12 October 2020}}</ref> The banded cotinga is not migratory and its nest is a simple cup.<ref name="datazone"/>

==Conservation==
The greatest threat to the banded cotinga's survival is deforestation. Continued [[habitat fragmentation]] has also complicated matters, sending populations into a sharp decline. Some protected areas, such as the [[RPPN Estação Veracel]] and [[Reserva Serra Bonita]], are now thought to serve as bastions for this species.<ref name="datazone"/>

zh.extract:
條紋傘鳥（Cotinga maculata），又名帶斑傘鳥，是巴西東南部特有的傘鳥。牠們棲息在熱帶低地的潮濕森林，但受到失去棲息地的威脅。目前被IUCN列为濒危物种。
其种加词“maculata”意为“有斑点的”。
